seoTitle: How to Create a Kanban Board in Jira
metaDescription: Set up a Kanban board in Jira from scratch to visualize and manage your team's ongoing work column by column.
intro: A Kanban board gives your team a live view of work moving from start to finish without the fixed timeboxes of Scrum sprints. This guide covers setting up a new Kanban board in Jira and getting it ready to use.

steps.heading: Start a new board
steps.body: From your project or the boards menu, choose to create a new board and select Kanban as the type.

steps.heading: Choose the project or filter
steps.body: Base the board on an existing project or a custom saved filter, depending on what work you want it to display.

steps.heading: Set up your columns
steps.body: Define columns that reflect your workflow stages, such as To Do, In Progress, Review, and Done.

steps.heading: Map statuses to columns
steps.body: Match each issue status to the correct column so cards land in the right place automatically.

steps.heading: Adjust card layout and settings
steps.body: Configure what information shows on each card, then start adding or moving issues onto the board.

faq.question: Do I need a separate project for a Kanban board?
faq.answer: No, you can build a Kanban board on top of an existing project or a filtered set of issues from multiple projects.

faq.question: Can I set a work-in-progress limit on columns?
faq.answer: Yes, Jira lets you set WIP limits per column to prevent too many issues from piling up in one stage.

faq.question: Is Kanban better than Scrum for every team?
faq.answer: It depends on your workflow; Kanban suits continuous flow work, while Scrum fits teams that plan in fixed sprints.

faq.question: Can I switch a Kanban board to Scrum later?
faq.answer: Board type is generally set at creation, so switching usually means creating a new board rather than converting the existing one.
